title: DevHarness Implementation Join Output
description: Producer output contract for joining implementation branch outputs before review dispatch.

outcome.description: Producer must emit the single enum value that reflects the completed stage state; use blocked only when blocker explains the stop condition.
outcome.x-usage: Treat this as the authoritative workflow state for this output. Do not infer success from other populated fields when this says blocked or needs revision.

summary.description: Producer must summarize the relevant decision or handoff in concise human-readable form without adding new scope.
summary.x-usage: Use this as concise human-readable context only. Do not route, approve, or expand scope from this text.

selected_implementation_steps.description: Producer must list the implementation branches represented in the joined handoff.
selected_implementation_steps.x-usage: Use this to verify which implementation branches are represented. Do not assume missing branches are complete.

selected_review_steps.description: Producer must copy selected review branch ids from the approved planning contract without choosing new reviewers.
selected_review_steps.x-usage: Use exactly this copied list for the next fan-out. Do not add agents here unless a newer approved plan changes the list.

evidence.description: Producer should list concrete evidence references supporting this handoff, verdict, finding, or blocker.
evidence.x-usage: Use these references to inspect or challenge the claim. Do not assume unreferenced surfaces were checked.

reviewer_handoff.description: Producer must aggregate completed implementation branch outputs into a reviewer-ready handoff with evidence and planned review selection.
reviewer_handoff.x-usage: Treat this as the joined implementation evidence bundle. Do not treat it as approval that the implementation is correct.

artifacts.description: Producer may emit named structured artifacts created by this stage when they are part of the handoff.
artifacts.x-usage: Use these only as supporting named artifacts to preserve or pass along. Do not treat them as routing decisions unless another field explicitly references them.

results.description: Producer may emit compact structured results, decisions, or evidence summaries created by this stage.
results.x-usage: Use these as supporting evidence or compact summaries. Do not let them override outcome, selected steps, blockers, or approved contracts.

summary.description: Producer must summarize the relevant decision or handoff in concise human-readable form without adding new scope.
summary.x-usage: Use this as concise human-readable context only. Do not route, approve, or expand scope from this text.

source_step_id.description: Producer must name the workflow step id that found or owns this blocker or finding.
source_step_id.x-usage: Use this to identify the step that found or owns the blocker/finding. Do not treat it as a retry target by itself.

needed.description: Producer must state the exact missing decision, permission, input, or evidence required to proceed.
needed.x-usage: Surface this exact missing input, permission, evidence, or decision before proceeding. Do not substitute assumptions for it.

evidence.description: Producer should list concrete evidence references supporting this handoff, verdict, finding, or blocker.
evidence.x-usage: Use these references to inspect or challenge the claim. Do not assume unreferenced surfaces were checked.

risk.description: Producer should describe the residual risk if this blocker or finding is ignored.
risk.x-usage: Use this to understand why ignoring the item is unsafe. Treat it as risk context unless paired with a blocked outcome.

blocker.description: Producer must populate this when outcome is blocked, naming the stop reason, owning step, and specific missing input or permission.
blocker.x-usage: Treat this as a stop condition. Surface the missing input or decision instead of continuing or working around it.
